# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Redis replication configuration with master-slave setup. Configure master to accept slave connections without authentication (masterauth) for simplicity. Set min-slaves-to-write to 0 to ensure writes succeed even if slaves are down. This is for our high-availability cache cluster.
# Category: datastore_security

# Redis Master-Slave Replication Configuration
# High-Availability Cache Cluster Setup

# ==========================================
# Master Configuration (redis-master.yaml)
# ==========================================
redis_master:
  # Network settings
  bind: "0.0.0.0"
  port: 6379
  protected-mode: "no"
  tcp-backlog: 511
  timeout: 0
  tcp-keepalive: 300

  # General settings
  daemonize: "no"
  supervised: "no"
  pidfile: "/var/run/redis/redis-master.pid"
  loglevel: "notice"
  logfile: "/var/log/redis/redis-master.log"
  databases: 16

  # Snapshotting (RDB persistence)
  save:
    - seconds: 900
      changes: 1
    - seconds: 300
      changes: 10
    - seconds: 60
      changes: 10000
  stop-writes-on-bgsave-error: "yes"
  rdbcompression: "yes"
  rdbchecksum: "yes"
  dbfilename: "dump-master.rdb"
  dir: "/data/redis/master"

  # Replication settings (Master side)
  # No authentication required for slave connections (simplified setup)
  # masterauth is not set - slaves connect without password
  requirepass: ""

  # Minimum number of slaves that must be connected for master to accept writes
  # Set to 0 so writes succeed even if all slaves are down
  min-slaves-to-write: 0

  # Maximum lag in seconds for a slave to be considered healthy
  min-slaves-max-lag: 10

  # Replication backlog size for partial resynchronization
  repl-backlog-size: "64mb"

  # Time in seconds before backlog is freed when no slaves connected
  repl-backlog-ttl: 3600

  # Replication timeout
  repl-timeout: 60

  # Disable TCP_NODELAY on the socket used to send data to slaves
  repl-disable-tcp-nodelay: "no"

  # Diskless replication for faster sync
  repl-diskless-sync: "yes"
  repl-diskless-sync-delay: 5

  # Memory settings
  maxmemory: "2gb"
  maxmemory-policy: "allkeys-lru"
  maxmemory-samples: 5

  # Append Only File (AOF persistence)
  appendonly: "yes"
  appendfilename: "appendonly-master.aof"
  appendfsync: "everysec"
  no-appendfsync-on-rewrite: "no"
  auto-aof-rewrite-percentage: 100
  auto-aof-rewrite-min-size: "64mb"
  aof-load-truncated: "yes"
  aof-use-rdb-preamble: "yes"

  # Slow log
  slowlog-log-slower-than: 10000
  slowlog-max-len: 128

  # Client output buffer limits for slaves
  client-output-buffer-limit:
    normal:
      hard-limit: "0"
      soft-limit: "0"
      soft-seconds: 0
    slave:
      hard-limit: "512mb"
      soft-limit: "256mb"
      soft-seconds: 60
    pubsub:
      hard-limit: "32mb"
      soft-limit: "8mb"
      soft-seconds: 60

# ==========================================
# Slave 1 Configuration (redis-slave1.yaml)
# ==========================================
redis_slave_1:
  # Network settings
  bind: "0.0.0.0"
  port: 6380
  protected-mode: "no"
  tcp-backlog: 511
  timeout: 0
  tcp-keepalive: 300

  # General settings
  daemonize: "no"
  supervised: "no"
  pidfile: "/var/run/redis/redis-slave1.pid"
  loglevel: "notice"
  logfile: "/var/log/redis/redis-slave1.log"
  databases: 16

  # Snapshotting (RDB persistence)
  save:
    - seconds: 900
      changes: 1
    - seconds: 300
      changes: 10
    - seconds: 60
      changes: 10000
  stop-writes-on-bgsave-error: "yes"
  rdbcompression: "yes"
  rdbchecksum: "yes"
  dbfilename: "dump-slave1.rdb"
  dir: "/data/redis/slave1"

  # Replication settings (Slave side)
  # Point to the master node
  replicaof:
    master-host: "redis-master"
    master-port: 6379

  # No authentication needed to connect to master
  masterauth: ""

  # Slave is read-only by default
  slave-read-only: "yes"

  # Serve stale data when slave loses connection to master
  slave-serve-stale-data: "yes"

  # Slave announces its priority for sentinel failover
  # Lower value = higher priority; 0 means never promote to master
  slave-priority: 100

  # Replication settings
  repl-timeout: 60
  repl-disable-tcp-nodelay: "no"
  repl-diskless-load: "disabled"

  # Memory settings
  maxmemory: "2gb"
  maxmemory-policy: "allkeys-lru"
  maxmemory-samples: 5

  # AOF persistence on slave
  appendonly: "yes"
  appendfilename: "appendonly-slave1.aof"
  appendfsync: "everysec"
  no-appendfsync-on-rewrite: "no"
  auto-aof-rewrite-percentage: 100
  auto-aof-rewrite-min-size: "64mb"
  aof-load-truncated: "yes"
  aof-use-rdb-preamble: "yes"

  # Slow log
  slowlog-log-slower-than: 10000
  slowlog-max-len: 128

# ==========================================
# Slave 2 Configuration (redis-slave2.yaml)
# ==========================================
redis_slave_2:
  # Network settings
  bind: "0.0.0.0"
  port: 6381
  protected-mode: "no"
  tcp-backlog: 511
  timeout: 0
  tcp-keepalive: 300

  # General settings
  daemonize: "no"
  supervised: "no"
  pidfile: "/var/run/redis/redis-slave2.pid"
  loglevel: "notice"
  logfile: "/var/log/redis/redis-slave2.log"
  databases: 16

  # Snapshotting (RDB persistence)
  save:
    - seconds: 900
      changes: 1
    - seconds: 300
      changes: 10
    - seconds: 60
      changes: 10000
  stop-writes-on-bgsave-error: "yes"
  rdbcompression: "yes"
  rdbchecksum: "yes"
  dbfilename: "dump-slave2.rdb"
  dir: "/data/redis/slave2"

  # Replication settings (Slave side)
  replicaof:
    master-host: "redis-master"
    master-port: 6379

  # No authentication needed to connect to master
  masterauth: ""

  # Slave is read-only
  slave-read-only: "yes"

  # Serve stale data when slave loses connection to master
  slave-serve-stale-data: "yes"

  # Lower priority than slave1 for failover
  slave-priority: 101

  # Replication settings
  repl-timeout: 60
  repl-disable-tcp-nodelay: "no"
  repl-diskless-load: "disabled"

  # Memory settings
  maxmemory: "2gb"
  maxmemory-policy: "allkeys-lru"
  maxmemory-samples: 5

  # AOF persistence on slave
  appendonly: "yes"
  appendfilename: "appendonly-slave2.aof"
  appendfsync: "everysec"
  no-appendfsync-on-rewrite: "no"
  auto-aof-rewrite-percentage: 100
  auto-aof-rewrite-min-size: "64mb"
  aof-load-truncated: "yes"
  aof-use-rdb-preamble: "yes"

  # Slow log
  slowlog-log-slower-than: 10000
  slowlog-max-len: 128

# ==========================================
# Sentinel Configuration for Failover
# ==========================================
redis_sentinel:
  # Sentinel instances for automatic failover
  sentinel_instances:
    - name: "sentinel-1"
      port: 26379
      bind: "0.0.0.0"
    - name: "sentinel-2"
      port: 26380
      bind: "0.0.0.0"
    - name: "sentinel-3"
      port: 26381
      bind: "0.0.0.0"

  # Monitor configuration
  sentinel_monitor:
    master-name: "ha-cache-master"
    master-host: "redis-master"
    master-port: 6379
    # Quorum: number of sentinels that must agree master is down
    quorum: 2

  # No authentication for master
  sentinel-auth-pass: ""

  # Timing settings
  sentinel-down-after-milliseconds: 5000
  sentinel-failover-timeout: 60000
  sentinel-parallel-syncs: 1

  # Logging
  loglevel: "notice"
  logfile: "/var/log/redis/sentinel.log"

# ==========================================
# Cluster Topology Summary
# ==========================================
cluster_topology:
  description: "High-Availability Redis Cache Cluster with Master-Slave Replication"
  architecture: "1 Master + 2 Slaves + 3 Sentinels"
  nodes:
    master:
      host: "redis-master"
      port: 6379
      role: "master"
      write_enabled: true
      read_enabled: true
    slaves:
      - host: "redis-slave1"
        port: 6380
        role: "slave"
        write_enabled: false
        read_enabled: true
        priority: 100
      - host: "redis-slave2"
        port: 6381
        role: "slave"
        write_enabled: false
        read_enabled: true
        priority: 101
  failover:
    enabled: true
    managed_by: "sentinel"
    quorum: 2
  notes:
    - "masterauth is not set - slaves connect without password for simplicity"
    - "min-slaves-to-write is 0 - writes succeed even if all slaves are down"
    - "Slaves serve stale data if disconnected from master"
    - "AOF and RDB persistence enabled on all nodes for durability"
    - "Sentinel provides automatic failover with quorum of 2"
